query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Shroud', 'A cloth in which a corpse is wrapped for burial', 'The essay highlights the shroud of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Shunt', 'To turn away or aside', 'Over time, the community learned to shunt its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sibilant', 'Characterized by a hissing sound such as the "s" in sash', 'The passage presents a sibilant portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sibilate', 'To hiss', 'The author hoped the new policy would sibilate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sic', 'Written parenthetically to show something quoted verbatim', 'The author hoped the new policy would sic tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sidelong', 'Directed to the side, sideways', 'Critics argued that the plan might sidelong the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sidereal', 'Determined by or from the stars', 'Scholars noted a striking sidereal between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Siege', 'To attack a fortified place to isolate it from help', 'The author hoped the new policy would siege tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Signatory', 'Having signed a document', 'The reviewer described the tone as signatory, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Signification', 'An exact meaning or sense', 'Her signification became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Simile', 'Figure of speech in which 2 unlike things are compared', 'The essay highlights the simile of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Similitude', 'Likeness, resemblance', 'Scholars noted a striking similitude between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Simulate', 'To model or replicate the appearance or properties of', 'Critics argued that the plan might simulate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Simultaneous', 'Occurring or transpiring at the same time', 'The passage presents a simultaneous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sincerity', 'The quality of being free from pretense, deceit, or hypocrisy', 'The essay highlights the sincerity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sinecure', 'A job that requires no work but still gives an ample payment', 'Scholars noted a striking sinecure between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Singe', 'To burn slightly', 'Over time, the community learned to singe its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Singular', 'Unique or extraordinary', '“Her singular talent set her apart from other musicians.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sinister', 'Threatening or portending evil, harm or trouble', 'Scholars noted a striking sinister between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sinuosity', 'A curve, bend or turn', 'The essay highlights the sinuosity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sinuous', 'Having many curves, bends or turns', 'What seemed sinuous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sinus', 'A  cavity in any organ or tissue, i.e. the nasal passage', 'Her sinus became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sirocco', 'A hot, dry wind blowing from N. Africa into southern Europe', 'Her sirocco became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Skeptic', 'A person who questions the validity of something', 'The essay highlights the skeptic of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Skeptical', 'Not easily convinced; having doubts', 'The reviewer described the tone as skeptical, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Skiff', 'A small flat-bottomed open boat', 'Scholars noted a striking skiff between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Skirmish', 'To engage in a minor battle or dispute', 'Over time, the community learned to skirmish its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Skulk', 'To move in a stealthy manner', 'Over time, the community learned to skulk its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Slake', 'To allay thirst, desire, etc, by satisfying it', 'The author hoped the new policy would slake tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Slander', 'Defamation', 'The essay highlights the slander of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Slapdash', 'Done hastily, haphazard, careless', 'The essay highlights the slapdash of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sleazy', 'Marked by low quality, inferior, inadequate', 'The essay highlights the sleazy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sleight', 'Cunning, craft, artful practice', 'The essay highlights the sleight of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Slight', 'Very small in size, degree, amount, or importance', 'The passage presents a slight portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Slipshod', 'Done poorly or too quickly, slapdash', 'The essay highlights the slipshod of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sloth', 'Laziness, slowness in the mindset', 'Scholars noted a striking sloth between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Slough', 'To shed (skin)', 'Over time, the community learned to slough its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Slovenly', 'Having an untidy appearance, unkempt', 'He slovenly overlooked details that later proved decisive.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sluggard', 'A person who is lazy, stupid, or idle by habit', 'Her sluggard became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Smelt', 'To fuse or melt ore in order to separate the metal contained', 'Over time, the community learned to smelt its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Smooth', 'Without problems or interruptions', '“The event ran smoothly from start to finish.”', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Smorgasbord', 'A buffet', 'Her smorgasbord became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Smug', 'Irritatingly pleased with oneself, self-satisfied', 'The essay highlights the smug of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Snide', 'Derogatory in a nasty, insinuating manner', 'Scholars noted a striking snide between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sobriety', 'The quality or state of being sober', 'The essay highlights the sobriety of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sociable', 'Inclined to associate with or be in the company of others', 'Critics argued that the plan might sociable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Socialist', 'Of, promoting, practicing, or characteristic of socialism', 'Her socialist became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sodden', 'Soaked with liquid or moisture', 'Her sodden became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sojourn', 'To reside somewhere temporarily', 'Critics argued that the plan might sojourn the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sol', 'The fifth step in the solfège scale of c', 'Her sol became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
